mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced household spending capacity, triggering immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand contraction, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive 60-day returns in over 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during high inflation periods eases supply chain bottlenecks and stabilizes input costs, supporting corporate margins and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth crossing below 1.5% signals economic stagnation and weak aggregate demand, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and driving negative monthly returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5% coincides with positive 20-day returns in over 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation shifts rather than sustainable job creation, weakening wage growth and producing negative quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent increases in PAYEMS and UNRATE yield positive 60-day returns in over 50% of periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales reflect robust consumer spending, directly boosting near-term corporate revenue expectations and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rising retail sales consistently precede negative 5-day returns in over 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside falling inflation during a tightening cycle confirms demand destruction rather than supply shocks, triggering recession fears and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Falling INDPRO and CPIAUCSL during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day returns in over 60% of instances.
